target.title: fix(qa): reduce WhatsApp live RTT setup cost
target.body:

- Skip WhatsApp live QA agent workspace bootstrap and force exact-marker canaries to run with thinking disabled in both defaults and per-model params.
- Add per-scenario WhatsApp live timings, RSS samples, complete heap snapshot capture, and redacted gateway trace artifacts.
- Skip eager participating-group metadata hydration for DM-only WhatsApp sessions.
- Add changelog coverage for the QA and WhatsApp runtime changes.

Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts`

- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx9ne1a4egr0qxpp22chkf5`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krxagz91ep6d1ca29qx5b00q`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0

Live evidence
- `26027068186` at `fdc2d0e9`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7532ms`; trace isolated ~`5141ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26027596921` at `74c4bb73`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7930ms`; trace isolated ~`5890ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26028278115` at `9ef7fbe1`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `10393ms`; trace isolated ~`7129ms` in agent/model before outbound send.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA canary artifacts now separate channel/provider cost from agent/model cost, capture complete heap snapshots, and avoid unnecessary setup/group hydration in the DM-only path.
Real environment tested: live WhatsApp Convex QA on GitHub Actions, plus Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa whatsapp --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/gpt-5.5 --alt-model openai/gpt-5.5 --scenario whatsapp-canary` through `qa-live-transports-convex.yml`.
Evidence after fix: WhatsApp provider-path trace is now visible; the latest run shows inbound/provider/outbound overhead is small compared with agent/model time.
Observed result after fix: this branch improves observability and removes some setup work, but it does not yet reduce the live canary into the target range; current dominant cost is agent/model pre-outbound latency.
What was not tested: full workflow green is not claimed; the exact workflow runs may show unrelated lane failures after the WhatsApp job/artifact succeeded.

target.title: fix(code-mode): honor agent scoped code mode
target.body:
Fixes #83388.

## Problem
`agents.list[].tools.codeMode` was documented as a per-agent setting, but main rejected it in the strict per-agent schema. A schema-only fix would be worse than the original rejection because it would accept security/tool-boundary config without making it effective at runtime.

## Change and value
- Accept `agents.list[].tools.codeMode` in schema, exported types, labels, and help text.
- Resolve code mode from the active agent, with agent settings overriding top-level `tools.codeMode` defaults.
- Wire the active-agent result through catalog activation and model payload enforcement so agent-scoped code mode is not accepted as inert config.
- Preserve provider grouped tool payloads, such as Gemini `functionDeclarations`, while filtering code-mode payloads down to `exec`/`wait`.

## Who is affected
Operators using per-agent code-mode policy now get the documented behavior. Existing top-level `tools.codeMode` behavior is preserved. Agents without code mode continue to see the normal tool surface.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Per-agent `agents.list[].tools.codeMode` changes the active agent's runtime/model tool surface; it is not merely accepted by schema validation.

Real environment tested: Local source worktree at rebased PR head `22c2376b7260`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Exercised the embedded-attempt runtime harness that captures the outgoing model request for an active `ops` agent, then ran a redacted transport harness against PR-head `resolveCodeModeConfig` and `createCodexNativeWebSearchWrapper` for `ops` and `chat`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
agent=ops resolvedCodeMode=true transportSurface=true payloadTools=exec,wait
agent=chat resolvedCodeMode=false transportSurface=false payloadTools=read,exec,wait
agent=ops resolvedCodeMode=true transportSurface=true payloadTools=exec,wait
```

Observed result after fix: The enabled `ops` agent sent only the code-mode payload tools (`exec`, `wait`); the disabled `chat` control kept the normal visible payload; and the grouped declaration case still filtered to `exec`, `wait`.

What was not tested: No external provider API request and no gateway process. This proof targets the embedded runner/model-transport boundary where accepted config becomes an outgoing tool payload.

## Verification
- Current head `22c2376b7260`: `env OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 180s node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-pi-embedded.config.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts` passed, 41 tests.
- Current head `22c2376b7260`: redacted model-transport proof harness output shown above.
- Current head `22c2376b7260`: `node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check` passed.
- Current head `22c2376b7260`: `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Earlier focused local Vitest for the unchanged runtime/config code passed: `zod-schema.agent-defaults.test.ts`, `code-mode.test.ts`, `openai-stream-wrappers.test.ts`, and `attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, 116 tests total.
- Pre-rebase validation on the same code diff: `oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>` passed.
- Pre-rebase validation on the same code diff: `timeout 900s codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable correctness issues.
- External Claude review was triaged: wrapper-stacking concern is rejected because `runEmbeddedAttempt` rebuilds from the session base stream before wrapping; shallow override and test-support notes are non-blocking for the current config shape/test harness.
- Pre-rebase remote GCP Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` on head `9b9cb2218662` passed, selected `core, coreTests, docs`, exit 0, lease `cbx_b35df657aaf8`.

target.title: Fix Codex runtime hook and session churn
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes two Codex-runtime stability problems reported by users:

- Fixes #76552: Codex no longer installs the default `PreToolUse` native hook relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, avoiding one `openclaw hooks relay ... --event pre_tool_use` child process per native Codex tool call.
- Fixes #76179: Codex no longer throws away the only saved app-server thread when the dynamic tool catalog changes. Instead, it keeps reusable Codex thread bindings per dynamic-tool fingerprint, so owner/non-owner tool availability flips start each catalog once and then resume the matching native thread.

## Root Cause

For #76552, current `main` already avoided relay subprocesses for `PostToolUse` and finalization hooks when no local handlers exist, but `pre_tool_use` still returned `true` unconditionally in `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts`. That meant default Codex app-server config could still register a `PreToolUse` command hook that shells out through the OpenClaw relay for every native tool use, even when there was no OpenClaw `before_tool_call` policy to run.

For #76179, the Codex app-server thread fingerprint included the dynamic tool registration set. Owner-only tools are filtered out for non-owner senders, so an owner/non-owner context change can alter the registered dynamic tool names. Before this PR, a mismatch cleared the single stored Codex binding, so alternating owner/non-owner turns could continuously create fresh native Codex sessions.

## What Changed

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.ts` now only reports local `pre_tool_use` relay work when `hasBeforeToolCallPolicy()` is true. `permission_request` remains active because Codex approvals still need the app-server bridge.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` can persist alternate Codex app-server thread bindings for the same OpenClaw session.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` selects a binding compatible with the current dynamic-tool fingerprint. If none exists, it starts a new binding without deleting other compatible bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` reselects the startup binding after dynamic tools are built, so context/startup decisions use the matching per-catalog Codex thread.
- Owner-only tools stay filtered for non-owner Codex thread starts. This avoids exposing owner-only schemas to non-owner turns.

## Before / After

Before:

- Default Codex sessions could register a `PreToolUse` relay command even with no `before_tool_call` plugin policy, so multiple native tools could fan out into multiple OpenClaw relay CLI child processes.
- Switching between owner and non-owner senders could change dynamic tool registration and repeatedly replace the saved Codex native thread binding.

After:

- No `PreToolUse` relay command is installed unless there is actual before-tool policy work.
- Command approvals still get the in-process `nativeHookRelay` bridge for `pre_tool_use` decisions.
- Owner-only schemas are not sent to non-owner Codex thread starts.
- Owner and non-owner dynamic-tool catalogs get separate reusable Codex thread bindings: first flip starts the second catalog, later flips resume the matching existing thread.

## Real Behavior Proof

- Behavior addressed: Codex runtime process/session churn. Default app-server runs should not install a `PreToolUse` relay command when no `before_tool_call` policy exists, and owner-only dynamic tool availability changes should not continuously replace Codex native sessions.
- Current head tested: `9f5c2eae749ff332c1b4dda50d7c04203968a4cf` on May 18, 2026.
- Real environment: Local macOS OpenClaw checkout, Node `v22.22.0`, Codex CLI logged in with ChatGPT auth. Private home/workspace paths and auth details are redacted in the older live-run transcript below.

### Production-code runtime probe on current head

This directly exercised the production `startOrResumeThread` and session-binding code on `9f5c2eae`, using owner tools `[message, cron]` and non-owner tools `[message]`:

```json
{
  "requestMethods": [
    "thread/start",
    "thread/start",
    "thread/resume",
    "thread/resume"
  ],
  "startDynamicTools": [
    [
      "message",
      "cron"
    ],
    [
      "message"
    ]
  ],
  "resumeThreadIds": [
    "thread-1",
    "thread-2"
  ],
  "storedBindings": [
    {
      "threadId": "thread-2",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    },
    {
      "threadId": "thread-1",
      "dynamicToolsFingerprintPresent": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result: the owner catalog and non-owner catalog start separate Codex threads once, then resume `thread-1` and `thread-2` on later flips. The non-owner `thread/start` payload only contained `message`, not `cron`.

### Live Codex app-server smoke

Earlier on this PR branch, I ran two real local Codex app-server OpenClaw turns with the same session id. That verified the actual app-server harness/auth path and same-thread resume behavior:

```text
$ codex login status
Logged in using ChatGPT

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK"

$ OPENCLAW_LOG_LEVEL=debug pnpm openclaw agent --local --model openai/gpt-5.4 --session-id codex-runtime-proof-83367 --message "Reply with exactly: OK2" --json --timeout 180
[plugins] loading codex from <repo>/dist/extensions/codex/index.js
[agents/auth-profiles] used external cli oauth bootstrap because local oauth was missing or unusable
[agent/embedded] strict-agentic execution contract active: runId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 sessionId=codex-runtime-proof-83367 provider=openai-codex/gpt-5.4 harness=codex configured=unspecified
[agents/harness] agent harness selected
...
"agentHarnessId": "codex"
"finalAssistantVisibleText": "OK2"
```

No product UI screenshot is applicable here because the bugs are runtime process/session churn in the Codex app-server path. Terminal/runtime proof is the relevant artifact.

## Tests / Proof

Current-head verification after the per-catalog binding update:

~~~text
$ git rev-parse HEAD
9f5c2eae749ff332c1b4dda50d7c04203968a4cf

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
# no output

$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.owner-only-gating.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  61 passed (61)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extensions.config.ts
 Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  177 passed (177)

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 49.77s

$ pnpm prompt:snapshots:check
Prompt snapshots are current (7 files).

$ env PATH=/opt/homebrew/bin:/Users/stainlu/.npm-global/bin:/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin pnpm check:test-types
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# exited 0
~~~

Specific regressions covered by this PR:

- `src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts`: no-handler relay state no longer enables `pre_tool_use`; `before_tool_call` still enables `PreToolUse`; `after_tool_call` does not accidentally enable `PreToolUse`.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.test.ts`: alternate dynamic-tool thread bindings persist beside the same OpenClaw session file.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts`: non-owner Codex thread starts omit owner-only dynamic tools, and owner/non-owner flips start each tool catalog once before resuming matching thread bindings.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/side-question.test.ts`: side-question/permission-request coverage keeps permission relay behavior while confirming default `PreToolUse` stays empty when no before-tool policy exists.

Broad Testbox `pnpm check:changed` was not run because the Blacksmith/Testbox CLI/env/profile were not available in this environment; the focused affected suites and the type-test gate above are green locally. The latest push also triggers exact-head GitHub CI.

target.title: fix(qqbot): respect OPENCLAW_HOME for media paths
target.body:
Fixes #83562.

## Summary
- Make QQ Bot data/media roots resolve from `OPENCLAW_HOME` when it is configured, matching OpenClaw core state path behavior.
- Keep existing `HOME` behavior as the fallback when `OPENCLAW_HOME` is not set.
- Add regression coverage proving a QQ Bot media file under `$OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/media/qqbot` is accepted by the structured payload local-file guard.

## Scout audit
- Audit A (existing helper): core has `resolveStateDir`/home resolution in `src/utils.ts`, but this extension helper avoids importing core internals; local path helper reused by existing QQ Bot data/media functions.
- Audit B (shared callers): `getQQBotDataPath`, `getQQBotMediaPath`, and shared media-root guard keep the same returned shape under `.openclaw`; only base home resolution changes when `OPENCLAW_HOME` is explicitly configured.
- Audit C (broader rival): no open rival PR found for `#83562` / `qqbot media OPENCLAW_HOME`.

## Validation
- `pnpm test extensions/qqbot/src/engine/utils/platform.test.ts` -> 9 passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `pnpm check:changed` -> passed

target.title: feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
target.body:
# PR 2 draft: Feishu reaction tool registration

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_reaction` tool with `add`, `remove`, and `list` actions.
- Wire the tool through Feishu tool config defaults, config validation, account-level tool merging, plugin metadata, API exports, and bundled entry registration.
- Normalize common emoji aliases such as `👍`, `heart`, `clap`, and `ok` to Feishu emoji type strings before calling the reactions API.
- Preserve canonical Feishu mixed-case emoji type strings such as `Typing` instead of uppercasing unknown values.
- Honor the existing `actions.reactions` policy gate as well as the new `tools.reactions` tool-registration gate.
- Document the Feishu-native reaction tool and `channels.feishu.tools.reactions` gate.

## Related upstream work

- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39567
- Addresses the missing reaction-tool portion of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8692
- Complements the lower-level reaction bug fixes in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478
- Keeps scope narrow and does not change the existing generic `message(action="react")` path, reducing overlap with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/34528 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Notes for reviewers

- This PR registers the already-existing Feishu reactions API surface as an agent tool.
- `tools.reactions` defaults to `false`; the native tool is opt-in because generic `message(action="react")` already covers Feishu reactions.
- `actions.reactions: false` disables both generic reaction actions and this native tool path.
- `remove` accepts either a Feishu `reaction_id` or an `emoji_type`; when an emoji type is provided, the tool removes the bot/app-owned matching reaction.
- Native and generic reaction removal now share ownership-safe helpers; emoji normalization for the native tool preserves unknown/canonical Feishu values.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can use the native `feishu_reaction` tool to add, list, and remove emoji reactions on real Feishu messages instead of having the reactions API implemented but unreachable from agents.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scope `im:message:reactions:write_only` enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Loaded the PR branch locally.
2. Read the existing Feishu bot config from `C:\Users\Clark\.openclaw\openclaw.json` without printing secrets.
3. Enabled `channels.feishu.tools.reactions: true` and `channels.feishu.actions.reactions: true` in memory for the live run.
4. Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` to import `extensions/feishu/src/reaction-tool.ts` from this branch, call `registerFeishuReactionTools(...)`, resolve the registered `feishu_reaction` tool, and execute `tool.execute(...)` against the live Feishu API.
5. Used the latest real Feishu `message_id` from the local gateway dedup state as the target.
6. Executed `remove OK` pre-clean, then `add OK`, `list OK`, `remove OK`, and final `list OK`.

Evidence after fix:
Copied terminal output from the live command. IDs are redacted; no app secret/token is included.

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "feishu_reaction add/list/remove live API proof",
  "source": "local OpenClaw PR branch tool.execute against Feishu API",
  "selectedMessage": {
    "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
    "dedupSeenAtMs": 1779090392068
  },
  "account": {
    "domain": "feishu",
    "appId": "cli_aa...2dcce",
    "toolsReactions": true,
    "actionsReactions": true
  },
  "assertions": {
    "ownAfterAdd": 1,
    "ownAfterRemove": 0
  },
  "transcript": [
    {
      "label": "pre-clean remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": false
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "add OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "add",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after add",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": [
          {
            "reactionId": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx",
            "emojiType": "OK",
            "operatorType": "app",
            "operatorId": "cli_aa...2dcce"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": {
          "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after remove",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": []
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: `add` returned a live `reaction_id`; `list` immediately returned one app-owned `OK` reaction whose `operatorId` matched the redacted app id; `remove` deleted that reaction; the final `list` returned an empty reaction list for `OK`.

What was not tested: visual screen recording of the Feishu chat UI; the proof above is copied terminal output from the real Feishu bot environment.

## Testing

```text
# 5 files, 73 tests passed

.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.cmd run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 778 tests passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.cmd --check ...
# passed

.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.cmd ...
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

target.title: fix(channels): hint at  when bundled channel module is missing
target.body:
### Summary

When a bundled channel plugin (e.g. `nostr`, `discord`, `whatsapp`, `msteams`) fails to load because its plugin-local runtime dependency hasn't been staged yet, the loader currently emits a bare warning like:

```
[channels] failed to load bundled channel nostr: Cannot find module 'nostr-tools'
```

This is alarming and not actionable — users have no signal that the supported repair path is `openclaw doctor --fix`, which is the design @steipete confirmed when closing #48803 ("doctor owns the repair path").

This PR detects `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` / `MODULE_NOT_FOUND` on the bundled channel load path and appends the canonical guidance to the warning detail, e.g.:

```
[channels] failed to load bundled channel nostr: Cannot find module 'nostr-tools' (run `openclaw doctor --fix` to install missing bundled runtime dependencies for channel nostr)
```

The hint phrasing matches existing usages in `src/plugins/discovery.ts`, `src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts`, and `src/cli/plugins-install-command.ts` so users see a consistent remediation surface.

### Why this approach

- **Maintainer-aligned** — does *not* mirror plugin runtime deps to the root `package.json` (which #48803 explicitly rejected); just makes the existing per-plugin staging design discoverable when it hasn't run yet.
- **Surgical** — pure UX/string change in one helper; behavior of the load path (warn-and-skip) is unchanged.
- **Symmetric** — applied to all seven `[channels] failed to load bundled channel ...` warn sites in `bundled.ts` (entry, setup entry, plugin, secrets, account inspector, setup plugin, setup secrets), so any of them produces the same friendly hint.

### Reproduction

`npm install -g openclaw@2026.5.2 && openclaw gateway restart` on a host that hasn't yet had `openclaw doctor --fix` stage the nostr plugin's runtime deps reproduces the warning.

### Tests

- `pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.channels.config.ts src/channels/plugins/bundled-root-caches.test.ts` — 4 passed (existing tests cover the load-error path mocked with `Cannot find module 'nostr-tools'`; my change only enriches the warning string and does not alter control flow).
- No assertions on the warning text exist anywhere in `src/`/`packages/`, so no test updates were required.

### Risk

Very low — appending to a warning string. No new exports, no public API, no control-flow changes. If `extractErrorCode` returns anything other than `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND`/`MODULE_NOT_FOUND` the original `formatErrorMessage(error)` output is returned unchanged.

### Changelog

Added under `## Unreleased > ### Fixes` per repo convention.

---

🤖 Drafted with assistance from GitHub Copilot CLI.

target.title: Separate prompt surfaces by selected harness
target.body:
## Summary

This PR separates runtime-specific prompt fragments by explicit prompt surface so PI, CLI, ACP, native Codex app-server, and subagent prompts do not inherit each other's assumptions.

The core issue is not that PI's prompt is wrong. PI can keep the full OpenClaw control prompt. The problem is that native Codex already has its own harness prompt stack, so OpenClaw must add only the parts it owns instead of accidentally layering a second PI/Codex policy prompt on top of Codex.

## Why This Update

After the first implementation, I ran a second adversarial audit against the actual Codex native app-server prompt stack. That audit confirmed the surface split is the right abstraction, and it found a few places where the PR description needed to be explicit about double-prompt risks:

- Codex native app-server runs layer OpenClaw `developerInstructions` on top of Codex model/base/personality instructions.
- PI prompt text is valid for PI, but becomes risky when copied into Codex native or CLI prompt lanes.
- Some OpenClaw overrides are intentional and should remain, especially source-channel reply delivery and Codex-vs-OpenClaw delegation routing.
- Some broader OpenClaw prompt/context material should be narrowed or handled as follow-up because it can compete with Codex-native AGENTS, skills, and personality handling.

## Prompt Surface Model

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Runtime["Selected harness/runtime"] --> Surface["AgentPromptSurfaceKind"]
  Surface --> PI["pi_main"]
  Surface --> CLI["cli_backend"]
  Surface --> ACP["acp_backend"]
  Surface --> Codex["codex_app_server"]
  Surface --> Subagent["subagent"]

  PI --> PIPrompt["Full OpenClaw/PI system prompt"]
  CLI --> CLIPrompt["CLI backend prompt without PI fallback text"]
  ACP --> ACPPrompt["ACP-specific guidance"]
  Codex --> CodexPrompt["Codex developerInstructions only"]
  Subagent --> SubPrompt["Subagent-scoped guidance only"]
```

The boundary this PR introduces is simple: prompt routing follows the selected harness/runtime prompt surface, not provider-string matching and not a single shared global string bucket.

## Native Codex Layering

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  CodexModel["Codex model/base instructions"] --> CodexTurn["Codex native turn context"]
  CodexPersonality["Codex personality/model messages"] --> CodexTurn
  CodexApps["Codex apps/skills/plugins instructions"] --> CodexTurn
  CodexAgents["Codex AGENTS.md/project docs as contextual user content"] --> CodexTurn

  OpenClawDev["OpenClaw developerInstructions"] --> CodexTurn
  OpenClawTools["OpenClaw dynamic tools"] --> CodexTurn
  UserPrompt["OpenClaw user/source prompt"] --> CodexTurn

  CodexTurn --> Model["Responses request"]
```

OpenClaw does not replace Codex `baseInstructions` in this PR. It uses Codex's `developerInstructions` lane for OpenClaw-owned behavior. That is the right lane, but it means we have to keep OpenClaw additions narrow.

## What Changes

- Adds a small prompt-surface helper and public plugin command guidance types for scoped agent prompt guidance.
- Keeps legacy `agentPromptGuidance: string[]` behavior while allowing structured `{ text, surfaces }` entries.
- Scopes Codex native command guidance to the main PI/OpenClaw agent prompt surface instead of subagent or CLI prompt lanes.
- Applies the same surface-scoped command guidance when compacted embedded runs rebuild their system prompt.
- Keeps PI's empty-tool fallback intact, while CLI backend prompts no longer mention PI or OpenClaw tool workflow hints when no OpenClaw tools are injected.
- Makes Codex app-server developer reply instructions conditional on `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, so automatic visible replies are not told to use `message` unconditionally.
- Keeps native Codex context priority sane by moving OpenClaw skills and non-`AGENTS.md` bootstrap context out of Codex app-server `developerInstructions` and into turn input reference context.
- Keeps Codex observability honest by using the same decorated turn prompt for `turn/start`, `llm_input`, trajectory `context.compiled`, and `prompt.submitted`.
- Incorporates adversarial-review hardening: structured prompt-surface validation now follows the file's lazy-init pattern for bundled startup safety, and subagent-scoped guidance renders even when ACP is disabled.

## Scenarios

| Scenario | Before | After / Intended Behavior |
| --- | --- | --- |
| PI has no explicit tool lines | PI fallback text is valid and should remain | PI still renders the OpenClaw tool fallback |
| CLI backend has no OpenClaw tools | CLI could inherit PI/OpenClaw fallback wording | CLI says no OpenClaw tool list is injected and uses only backend tools |
| Subagent prompt is built | Global Codex command guidance could appear in child prompts | Subagent receives only subagent-scoped guidance |
| Native Codex automatic source reply | Codex could be told to use `message` even when Codex delivery is automatic | Automatic mode says to use the active Codex delivery path |
| Native Codex `message_tool_only` source reply | Codex final-answer habit can conflict with OpenClaw channel delivery | OpenClaw intentionally overrides and tells Codex to use `message` |
| Native Codex delegation | Codex native `spawn_agent` and OpenClaw `sessions_spawn` can compete | Codex subagents use native `spawn_agent`; OpenClaw/ACP delegation uses `sessions_spawn` |
| ACP backend prompt | ACP-specific docs/snapshots can become the oracle for Codex app-server behavior | ACP remains its own prompt surface and does not define native Codex behavior |

## Codex Conflict Audit

These are the specific conflict classes checked against the actual Codex native prompt stack:

### 1. Codex base/personality versus OpenClaw GPT-5 overlay

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  A["Codex base/model/personality prompt"] --> C["Native Codex run"]
  B["OpenClaw GPT-5 behavior/personality overlay"] --> C
  C --> Risk["Risk: duplicate style/execution policy"]
```

Severity: high for native Codex if left broad.

Recommendation: native Codex should let Codex own base/model/personality behavior. OpenClaw should keep only OpenClaw-owned routing, channel delivery, and runtime guidance in `developerInstructions`.

### 2. Visible reply delivery versus Codex final-answer behavior

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
  participant User
  participant OpenClaw
  participant Codex
  participant Channel

  User->>OpenClaw: Source-channel request
  OpenClaw->>Codex: developerInstructions with delivery mode
  alt message_tool_only
    Codex->>OpenClaw: message(action=send)
    OpenClaw->>Channel: visible reply
  else automatic
    Codex->>OpenClaw: normal Codex delivery path
    OpenClaw->>Channel: route once
  end
```

Severity: medium-high, but intentional.

Recommendation: keep the OpenClaw override only for `message_tool_only`. Automatic mode should stay neutral, which this PR now enforces.

### 3. Codex native subagents versus OpenClaw sessions

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  Ask["Delegation request"] --> IsCodex{"Codex-native subagent?"}
  IsCodex -->|Yes| Spawn["Use Codex spawn_agent"]
  IsCodex -->|No, OpenClaw or ACP session| Sessions["Use OpenClaw sessions_spawn"]
```

Severity: medium-high, but intentional.

Recommendation: keep this disambiguation in the Codex app-server developer instructions. Without it, Codex sees two delegation surfaces and can choose the wrong one.

### 4. Lightweight Codex context versus AGENTS.md

```mermaid
flowchart LR
  Light["OpenClaw lightweight context"] --> Config["project_doc_max_bytes = 0"]
  Config --> CodexDocs["Codex native AGENTS.md loading disabled"]
  Bootstrap["OpenClaw bootstrap text"] --> Claim["Claims Codex loads AGENTS.md natively"]
  CodexDocs --> Conflict["Conflict"]
  Claim --> Conflict
```

Severity: high when lightweight mode is used.

Recommendation: do not claim Codex loads AGENTS.md if the thread config disables project docs, or inject equivalent scoped AGENTS content when lightweight mode intentionally suppresses native docs. This is a prompt-surface correctness issue, not a PI prompt issue.

### 5. Skills and user-editable context priority

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  OpenClawSkills["OpenClaw skillsSnapshot.prompt"] --> Dev["Codex developerInstructions"]
  CodexSkills["Codex native skills instructions"] --> DevBundle["Codex developer bundle"]
  WorkspaceContext["SOUL/MEMORY/HEARTBEAT/project files"] --> Dev
  Dev --> Risk["Risk: duplicated or elevated user-editable context"]
  DevBundle --> Risk
```

Severity: medium.

Recommendation: keep OpenClaw-only context, but avoid turning generic user-editable context into Codex developer policy unless that priority is intentional. This is a good follow-up area after the prompt-surface routing split is in place.

### 6. Legacy plugin guidance compatibility

Severity: medium-low.

Legacy string guidance remains global for backwards compatibility. New structured entries can be scoped by surface. This PR preserves old plugin behavior while giving new prompt callers a precise path.

## Scope Boundary

This PR should be reviewed as the prompt-surface split:

- In scope: explicit prompt surface kinds, scoped plugin command guidance, PI/CLI/subagent/Codex developer-instruction routing, and visible-reply delivery wording.
- In scope to harden before merge if needed: any direct contradiction that makes a non-PI surface render PI/OpenClaw workflow text.
- Follow-up: deeper native Codex context priority decisions that are not required for this split, including any decision to suppress OpenClaw GPT-5 personality overlay on native Codex by default.
- Out of scope: harness selection policy, model routing, auth profile routing, Codex app-server transport behavior, ACP runtime behavior, and GitNexus indexing freshness.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: prompt routing now follows the selected runtime prompt surface instead of leaking PI/Codex/CLI/subagent assumptions through shared strings.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source worktree at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, branch `codex/prompt-surface-runtime-separation`, using the repo's TypeScript runtime loader (`node --import tsx`) against the actual prompt builders.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
pwd
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { buildAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/system-prompt.ts';
import { buildCliAgentSystemPrompt } from './src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.ts';
import { resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey } from './src/agents/prompt-surface.ts';
import { buildDeveloperInstructions } from './extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts';
import { clearPluginCommands, registerPluginCommand } from './src/plugins/commands.ts';

const workspaceDir = process.cwd();
clearPluginCommands();
registerPluginCommand('probe-plugin', {
  name: 'probe_cmd',
  description: 'Probe command',
  agentPromptGuidance: [
    { text: 'PI main probe guidance.', surfaces: ['pi_main'] },
    { text: 'CLI backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['cli_backend'] },
    { text: 'Codex app-server probe guidance.', surfaces: ['codex_app_server'] },
    { text: 'ACP backend probe guidance.', surfaces: ['acp_backend'] },
    { text: 'Subagent probe guidance.', surfaces: ['subagent'] },
  ],
  handler: async () => ({ text: 'ok' }),
});
const piPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'pi_main', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['PI main probe guidance.'] });
const cliPrompt = buildCliAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, tools: [], modelDisplay: 'test/model' });
const acpSurface = resolveAgentPromptSurfaceForSessionKey('agent:codex:acp:probe');
const acpPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: acpSurface, nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['ACP backend probe guidance.'] });
const subagentPrompt = buildAgentSystemPrompt({ workspaceDir, toolNames: [], promptSurface: 'subagent', nativeCommandGuidanceLines: ['Subagent probe guidance.'] });
const baseCodexParams = {
  agentDir: workspaceDir,
  workspaceDir,
  provider: 'openai-codex-responses',
  modelId: 'gpt-5.4-codex',
  config: {},
  prompt: 'probe prompt',
};
const messageToolOnlyDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'message_tool_only',
});
const automaticDeveloper = buildDeveloperInstructions({
  ...baseCodexParams,
  sourceReplyDeliveryMode: 'automatic',
});

const probe = {
  cwd: workspaceDir,
  piHasPiFallback: piPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasPiFallback: cliPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  cliHasBackendFallback: cliPrompt.includes('No OpenClaw tool list is injected for this runtime prompt surface'),
  cliHasLongWaitHint: cliPrompt.includes('For long waits'),
  cliHasSessionsSpawnHint: cliPrompt.includes('Larger work: use `sessions_spawn`'),
  cliHasPollingHint: cliPrompt.includes('Do not poll `subagents list` / `sessions_list` in a loop'),
  cliHasCliScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('CLI backend probe guidance.'),
  cliHasPiScopedGuidance: cliPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpSurfaceFromSessionKey: acpSurface,
  acpHasAcpScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('ACP backend probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiScopedGuidance: acpPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  acpHasPiFallback: acpPrompt.includes('Pi lists the standard tools above'),
  subagentHasSubagentGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('Subagent probe guidance.'),
  subagentHasPiGuidance: subagentPrompt.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage: messageToolOnlyDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticSaysUseMessage: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies: use `message`'),
  automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath: automaticDeveloper.includes('Visible channel replies should use the active Codex delivery path'),
  codexHasCodexScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Codex app-server probe guidance.'),
  codexHasPiScopedGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('PI main probe guidance.'),
  codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance: automaticDeveloper.includes('Use Codex native `spawn_agent` for Codex subagents'),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(probe, null, 2));
clearPluginCommands();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix: terminal output from the live prompt probe:

```text
/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation
{
  "cwd": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation",
  "piHasPiFallback": true,
  "cliHasPiFallback": false,
  "cliHasBackendFallback": true,
  "cliHasLongWaitHint": false,
  "cliHasSessionsSpawnHint": false,
  "cliHasPollingHint": false,
  "cliHasCliScopedGuidance": true,
  "cliHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpSurfaceFromSessionKey": "acp_backend",
  "acpHasAcpScopedGuidance": true,
  "acpHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "acpHasPiFallback": false,
  "subagentHasSubagentGuidance": true,
  "subagentHasPiGuidance": false,
  "messageToolOnlySaysUseMessage": true,
  "automaticSaysUseMessage": false,
  "automaticUsesActiveDeliveryPath": true,
  "codexHasCodexScopedGuidance": true,
  "codexHasPiScopedGuidance": false,
  "codexHasNativeSpawnAgentGuidance": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: the rendered PI prompt still includes the PI empty-tool fallback; the rendered CLI backend prompt does not include PI wording or OpenClaw long-wait/session-spawn/polling workflow hints when no tool list is injected; ACP session keys resolve to the `acp_backend` surface and render ACP-scoped guidance without PI guidance or fallback text; CLI, subagent, and Codex app-server scoped command guidance render only on their matching surfaces; Codex `message_tool_only` developer instructions still say to use `message`, while Codex `automatic` developer instructions use the active delivery path instead of a hard `message` instruction.

What was not tested: full repository test suite, live Codex app-server connection to Codex Desktop, GitNexus refreshed index.

## Folded Codex Context Priority Fix

The brief #83555 draft follow-up was folded back into this PR. The original prompt-surface split remains the canonical change set, and the folded commits address the Codex-native double-prompt concern directly:

- OpenClaw skills and non-`AGENTS.md` bootstrap context no longer enter Codex app-server `developerInstructions`.
- Native Codex still receives that OpenClaw context in the lower-priority turn input reference lane.
- Lightweight cron/project-doc suppression remains intact.
- The decorated turn prompt is the prompt reported to `turn/start`, `llm_input`, trajectory `context.compiled`, and `prompt.submitted`.

Additional local validation after the fold-in:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/context-engine-projection.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Results: 199 tests passed, 126 tests passed, 4 focused Vitest shards passed, and `git diff --check` passed from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`.

## Post-Rebase Update

Rebased onto upstream `main` at `419eea24622b8649dd98155734a01f972463e300`, including upstream #83561 (`fix(codex): stop forcing code-mode-only turns`). This PR preserves that upstream behavior: Codex code mode is enabled, `features.code_mode_only` defaults to `false`, and explicit thread config can still opt into code-mode-only.

Current head proof after rebase:

```text
OpenClaw prompt-surface Codex context proof
{
  "cwd": "/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation",
  "head": "41b35f4799e4",
  "developerContainsOpenClawSkills": false,
  "developerContainsOpenClawWorkspaceContext": false,
  "developerVisibleReplyInstruction": "Preserve channel/session context. Visible channel replies should use the active Codex delivery path; do not describe would-reply.",
  "sourceBuildsTurnInputContextFromSkills": true,
  "sourcePrependsOpenClawContextToTurnInput": true,
  "sourceKeepsDecoratedPromptObservable": true,
  "sourceSkipsOpenClawContextForLightweightCron": true,
  "reportCountsDemotedSkillsOnlyWhenInjected": true
}
```

Additional post-rebase validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-prompt-surface-runtime-separation`:

```bash
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts
OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --fileParallelism=false src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
DOCS_I18N_GLOSSARY_BASE=upstream/main pnpm check:docs
pnpm format:docs:check
node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs
git diff --check
```

Results: 200 Codex app-server tests passed, 234 prompt/registry/compaction tests passed, docs check passed against upstream main, docs formatting passed, conflict-marker check passed, and `git diff --check` passed.

## Verification

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/commands.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner/helpers.system-prompt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts
git diff --check
```

- Focused prompt/registry/compaction/Codex/plugin-SDK tests passed 5 Vitest shards in 31.68s after the adversarial-review hardening fixes.
- `git diff --check` passed.

## Adversarial Sweep

Four read-only adversarial review passes covered prompt-surface correctness, lifecycle/compaction routing, tests/mocks, and public SDK/API compatibility. Two findings survived manual verification and were fixed in this head:

- P1 bundled startup safety: avoid a module-scope prompt-surface `Set` in `command-registration.ts`, matching the file's existing lazy-init TDZ guard pattern.
- P2 subagent guidance placement: render subagent-scoped command guidance independently of ACP-specific routing text, with regression coverage for ACP-disabled subagent prompts.

A second Codex-native conflict audit compared OpenClaw's Codex bridge to the actual Codex app-server prompt stack. That audit produced the scenarios documented above: native Codex owns base/model/personality/AGENTS/native skills, while OpenClaw should own delivery routing, dynamic-tool routing, ACP/session disambiguation, and genuinely OpenClaw-only context.

target.title: fix(onboard): fall back to fs.rm when 'trash' command is unavailable (#83459)
target.body:
Fixes #83459.

`moveToTrash` in `src/commands/onboard-helpers.ts:248` invoked the `trash` command and, on failure, logged `Failed to move to Trash (manual delete): <path>` without actually deleting the path. In the official Docker image — which intentionally keeps default apt packages minimal (extras now added via `OPENCLAW_IMAGE_APT_PACKAGES` per the recent `47b8e56e3f feat(docker): add image apt package build arg`) — `trash-cli` is not installed, so `openclaw agents delete`, the doctor reset paths, and other callers silently leaked workspace/agents/sessions directories.

This patch adds an `fs.rm({ recursive: true, force: true })` fallback inside the catch path. On systems where `trash` IS available the happy path is unchanged; on systems where it isn't, the path is now actually removed and the log message surfaces the distinction (`Deleted (trash unavailable): ...`). If `fs.rm` also fails (e.g. `EACCES` on a read-only mount), the actual error is surfaced so operators can repair manually.

Intentionally **not** modifying the Dockerfile — the recent `47b8e56e3f` change moved away from baking extras into the default image, so adding `trash-cli` to the default `apt-get install` set would fight that direction. The code-side fallback fixes the bug in every environment uniformly, including non-Docker minimal Linux installs that hit the same gap.

## Changes

- `src/commands/onboard-helpers.ts`: in `moveToTrash`, return on trash success; in the catch path, attempt `fs.rm({ recursive, force })` and log the trash-vs-rm distinction; if that also fails, surface the error message. 8-line comment block referencing #83459 + the recent Dockerfile policy direction.
- `src/commands/onboard-helpers.test.ts`: 3 new regression cases (fallback removes path when trash rejects; happy-path trash success doesn't trigger fallback log; degraded both-fail surfaces error).

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw agents delete bug_test --force` in the official Docker image (`v2026.5.12`) logged `Failed to move to Trash (manual delete): /tmp/bug_test` and left the directory on disk, leaving residual files in `workspace` and `agents` after the agent was "deleted" from `openclaw.json`. The fallback restores the semantic contract.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83459.mjs` exercises the OLD and NEW `moveToTrash` shapes with real `fs` operations on a `mkdtemp` scratch tree, using a stubbed `runTrash` that always rejects with `ENOENT` (the actual error shape the official Docker image produces).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83459.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
=== Case A: OLD code on missing trash-cli (bug repro) ===
[OLD log] Failed to move to Trash (manual delete): /tmp/openclaw-probe-83459-.../old-agent
PASS: OLD leaves directory on disk (BUG CONFIRMED)

=== Case B: NEW code on missing trash-cli (fix) ===
[runtime.log] Deleted (trash unavailable): /tmp/openclaw-probe-83459-.../new-agent
PASS: NEW falls back to fs.rm and removes directory

=== Case C: NEW code on already-missing path (no-op) ===
PASS: NEW returns silently when path doesn't exist

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** When `trash` is missing, the path is actually deleted (the bug surface is closed). When `trash` is present, behavior is unchanged. When neither works (e.g. read-only mount), the failure is surfaced with the actual error message instead of producing a silent leak. The pre-existing fast-return for already-missing paths is preserved.

- **What was not tested:** Live invocation through `openclaw agents delete` against the actual `openclaw/openclaw:v2026.5.12` Docker container. The new vitest cases cover the public path through `moveToTrash` directly with mocked `runCommandWithTimeout` to simulate the missing-binary failure; the probe verifies the semantic contract with real `fs` operations.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — including new prototype-pollution + recent-merge steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `runCommandWithTimeout` and `fs.rm` (both already in use). No new helper. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** `moveToTrash` is exported with 5 prod callers (`handleReset`, `agents.commands.delete.ts` × 3, etc.). The function signature is unchanged; the behavior is strictly improved (success cases match prior; failure cases now actually delete instead of leaking). PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs touching `moveToTrash` / `trash-cli` / Dockerfile-default-trash. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit (new):** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/commands/onboard-helpers.ts Dockerfile` shows `47b8e56e3f feat(docker): add image apt package build arg` just landed on Dockerfile — that PR's direction (operator-driven extras, not baked-in default) is *why* this code-side fix is the right shape rather than a Dockerfile change. No recent merges on `onboard-helpers.ts`. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — no external-input key copying.

target.title: Fix Telegram topic media completion delivery
target.body:
## Summary
- stringify Telegram/forum thread ids before requester-agent completion handoff calls the gateway `agent` method
- accept direct in-process gateway agent payloads as delivery evidence, not only wrapped `{ result }` responses
- add regression coverage for generated video completion in a Telegram topic with message-tool media evidence

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Generated video completion delivery into a Telegram forum topic failed after the video finished because requester-agent completion handoff passed numeric `threadId` to the gateway `agent` method, which requires a string.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway connected to Telegram topic `telegram:-1003970070733`, with `xai/grok-imagine-video` generating a video completion.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the fix locally, restarted the gateway, triggered generated-video task `3c71c210-5cf0-4ad9-ace4-82c73b04eab4`, and let the requester-agent completion deliver through the Telegram message tool.

Evidence after fix: Telegram outbound log reported `telegram outbound send ok accountId=default chatId=-1003970070733 messageId=138 operation=sendVideo deliveryKind=video`; generated file `video-1---c6d8cbae-12e1-452b-b9f4-9b3c7fe631aa.mp4` probed as valid H.264 video, 1280x720, 5.041667s, 5,902,659 bytes.

Observed result after fix: Telegram delivery succeeded at `2026-05-18 04:09 MDT`; user-provided Telegram screenshot showed the video rendered in the topic with the expected caption.

What was not tested: Maintainer-controlled Telegram Desktop recording was not captured; the PR body includes runtime log and screenshot-described proof instead.

## Before-fix evidence
- Task `4a0f1eac-d323-431c-b36d-38682650c4b8` generated a video with `xai/grok-imagine-video`, but completion delivery failed with `invalid agent params: at /threadId: must be string`.

## Verification
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts -t "stringifies Telegram topic ids for generated video completion handoff"`
- `pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-route.test.ts src/channels/conversation-resolution.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/schema/agent.test.ts`
- `pnpm build`

## Known unrelated current-main failure
- `pnpm test src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` still fails one existing expectation, `requires message-tool delivery for channel subagent completions`; the same targeted test also fails on clean `main`.

target.title: fix(gateway): emit session_start hook on agent.send auto-rotation (#83507)
target.body:
Fixes #83507.

`agent.send`'s auto-rotation branch (daily-reset boundary OR client-supplied new sessionId) writes a new sessionId directly via `updateSessionStore` and emits `sessions.changed reason="create"` — but **bypasses** `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook`. Every other session-creation path emits the hook:

- `sessions.reset` → `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts:836`
- `sessions.create` (with `emitCommandHooks=true`) → `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts:1326`
- `/new` and `/reset` slash commands in agent input → same canonical path

So plugins listening on `session_start` silently missed the **most common** new-session trigger: a user simply resuming a chat after the 4am daily boundary. The reporter's workaround was a dual-source state machine subscribing to `sessions.subscribe` and treating `reason="create"` as a synonymous trigger.

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: import `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `../session-reset-service.js` (already had `performGatewaySessionReset` from the same module); call it immediately after the existing `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` inside the `if (requestedSessionKey && resolvedSessionKey && isNewSession)` branch. Argument shape matches the canonical `sessions.reset` site (`cfg`, `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, `storePath`, `sessionFile`, `agentId`).
- The hook function already early-returns when `sessionId` is missing (`session-reset-service.ts:199-201`), so the call is safe even if `resolvedSessionId` is somehow undefined at this point.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Plugins registered for `session_start` were not invoked when `agent.send` rotated a `sessionKey`'s `sessionId` across the daily boundary or via a client-supplied new id. The fix makes the hook fire in lockstep with the existing `sessions.changed reason="create"` event for that branch.

- **Real environment tested:** Local Node 22.x. Probe at `/tmp/probe_83507.mjs` parses the patched `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` and the unchanged `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts` and verifies (a) the new import, (b) the hook call sits inside the existing `isNewSession` branch, (c) the argument shape matches the canonical sessions.reset call site, (d) the hook's existing `sessionId` guard is preserved.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node /tmp/probe_83507.mjs`

- **Evidence after fix:**

```
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook imported
PASS: emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook called inside the isNewSession branch
PASS: new hook call passes cfg/sessionKey/sessionId/storePath/sessionFile/agentId
PASS: session-reset-service.ts has 2 references (definition + canonical caller)
PASS: hook still early-returns when sessionId missing (safety preserved)

ALL CASES PASS
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Source-level the new emit site is structurally equivalent to the existing `sessions.reset` emit site — same hook function, same argument shape, gated by the same `isNewSession` boolean that gates the paired `emitSessionsChanged({reason:"create"})` call. Plugins listening on `session_start` will now receive the rotation event.

- **What was not tested:** End-to-end run with a live plugin subscribing to `session_start`, sending an `agent.send` across the daily reset, and observing the hook fire. No live gateway available in this environment. The existing giant `agent.test.ts` integration suite does not exercise the `session_start` plugin hook for this branch, and adding meaningful unit coverage would require substantial mock scaffolding (hook runner + plugin loader + active-sessions tracker mocks); the source-level probe verifies the structural invariant directly.

## Audit (per CLAUDE rules — all 5 steps)

- **Existing-helper check:** Reuses `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` from `session-reset-service.js` (the canonical hook emitter the reset path already uses). No new function. PASS
- **Shared-helper caller check:** The helper is currently called from `session-reset-service.ts:836` (the reset path) and tested in `drain-active-sessions-for-shutdown.test.ts`. Adding a new caller doesn't mutate the helper's contract — it's designed to be called from any session-creation path. PASS
- **Broader-fix rival scan:** No open PRs touching `agent.ts` auto-rotation / `emitGatewaySessionStartPluginHook` / `session_start` hook missing-on-rotation. PASS
- **Recent-merge audit:** `git log --oneline -10 -- src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` shows `91f45d9c8a fix(gateway): dedupe exec followup continuations (#82717)` — unrelated. No recent merges that would conflict. PASS
- **Prototype-pollution scan:** N/A — no external-input key copying.

target.title: feat(anthropic): claude-cli-interactive backend — stream reasoning via local TLS proxy (experimental)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem.** OpenClaw's `claude-cli` backend invokes `claude -p --output-format stream-json` (headless). Starting **June 15, 2026** that path moves onto Anthropic's separate programmatic credit pool ([VentureBeat coverage](https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-reinstates-openclaw-and-third-party-agent-usage-on-claude-subscriptions-with-a-catch)) — fixed monthly allowance, billed at API rates, doesn't roll over.
- **Why it matters.** Every OpenClaw Opus turn on a Claude subscription will drain that fixed credit post–June 15 instead of counting against the interactive subscription limits subscribers are paying for. Opus turns exhaust the credit fast.
- **What changed.** New opt-in `claude-cli-interactive` backend. Each turn spawns one fresh `bun wrapper.ts …` that boots a loopback HTTPS MITM on a 127.0.0.1 port, taps every API SSE event, re-emits the events as `claude -p --output-format stream-json` JSONL records, and kills the claude child on `end_turn`. A `tty-spoof.cjs` `NODE_OPTIONS=--require` preload keeps claude in interactive mode under piped stdio.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary).** Existing `claude-cli` backend untouched. `agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli` user overrides inherit into the interactive backend via `inheritUserConfigFrom` with `-p`-mode flags stripped. System CA store is not mutated (`NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` scoped to the spawned claude child only). Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels pass through unmodified. Tool-use rendering / waiting-on-tool injection lands in #82285, not here.
- **AI-assisted:** yes — drafted with Claude.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `claude` running under OpenClaw in interactive (subscription) mode with full `init` / `stream_event*` / `result` JSONL reconstruction on the wrapper's stdout, sourced from the loopback MITM proxy's SSE tap.
- **Real environment tested:** Windows 11 Pro 26200, Node v24.12.0, pnpm, Bun, `@anthropic-ai/claude-code` v2.1.142 (npm-installed), Opus 4.7 (`claude-opus-4-7`), live OpenClaw gateway driving the backend per-turn over Telegram.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `unset NODE_OPTIONS && pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`; `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/anthropic/tsconfig.json`; set `cliBackends.claude-cli-interactive` active in user config; restart the OpenClaw gateway; send a Telegram message that triggers an Opus 4.7 turn; observe wrapper boot in stderr, SSE flow through `127.0.0.1:<port>`, JSONL arriving at OpenClaw; send a follow-up message with `--resume <sessionId>` to verify resume.
- **Evidence after fix:**
  - vitest: `Test Files 1 passed (1) / Tests 5 passed (5)` — `normalizeConfig` branches (inherited claude-cli match, direct Bun-path override, env passthrough, no-override skip).
  - Gateway log excerpt + cert listing + active-config snapshot on the [`proof-assets/pr-81851`](https://github.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/tree/proof-assets/pr-81851/.proof) branch. Embedded sample (one of three real turns in the gateway-log excerpt):

    ```
    cli exec: provider=claude-cli-interactive model=opus useResume=true session=present
    cli argv: bun <NPM>\…\wrapper.ts --effort max --verbose --setting-sources user --resume <SESSION> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__* --model opus <prompt:N chars>
    [openclaw-proxy] certs ready: <HOME>\.openclaw\proxy-certs\ca.crt
    [openclaw-proxy] MITM proxy active on 127.0.0.1:17081 — Anthropic API traffic intercepted locally to capture thinking_delta events
    [openclaw-proxy] spawning claude binary: <NPM>\…\@anthropic-ai\claude-code\bin\claude.exe
    [openclaw-proxy] result flushed, waiting for session write before kill
    [openclaw-proxy] session file updated, killing claude
    ```
- **Observed result after fix:** per-turn lifecycle terminates cleanly — wrapper emits `init`, a continuous stream of `stream_event` records, then a single `result` on `end_turn`, sets `intentionalKill = true`, and `claude.kill()`s the child so the per-turn process tree exits 0.
- **What was not tested:** Linux/macOS (developed on Windows 11 only); bundled-MCP `claude-config-file` mode end-to-end with multiple MCP servers active (only `mcp__openclaw__*` exercised live); forced Anthropic-side `429` / `overloaded_error` mid-stream (the `api_error` synthetic event path is implemented but I did not provoke a real 429).

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Integrations

## Linked

- Related: #81550 (programmatic-mode reasoning bridge — this PR is the interactive-mode equivalent on a separate backend); #82285 (tool-use injection, builds on the `stream_event` surface this PR establishes).

## Root Cause

N/A — feature PR. The lack of thinking visibility on subscription is a property of `claude -p` (headless) mode, not a regression in OpenClaw.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend-interactive.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `normalizeConfig` post-merge shape across four branches — inherited claude-cli match (forces `command="bun"`, stashes binary on env), direct Bun-path override (keeps the override as the spawn command, no env stash), env passthrough preservation, no-override case (env passthrough omitted so wrapper falls back to PATH lookup of `"claude"`).
- **Why smallest reliable guardrail:** the failure mode is config-shape drift inside `normalizeConfig` — fully exercised synchronously without spawning bun or claude, runs in ms vs the minutes an e2e run would cost.

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** newly-installed user with no inherited `claude-cli` override could spawn `bun` as the claude binary if `normalizeConfig` stashed `merged.command` indiscriminately.
  - **Mitigation:** `normalizeConfig` consults `context.config.agents.defaults.cliBackends.claude-cli.command`; only stashes the env passthrough on a match. Regression test asserts the env var is `undefined` for the no-override branch.
- **Risk:** `tty-spoof.cjs` is a runtime support file (not a build output) and could be omitted from packaged installs.
  - **Mitigation:** declared in `extensions/anthropic/package.json#openclaw.build.staticAssets`; postbuild asset copier ships it alongside the wrapper.
- **Risk:** winget-installed `claude.exe` shadows the npm install via PATHEXT on Windows.
  - **Mitigation:** wrapper spawns `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_CLAUDE_BINARY` directly when the parent backend resolved a binary, never falling back to a bare PATH lookup.
- **Risk:** title-gen / Haiku side-requests inject `{"title":"…"}` artifacts into the reasoning stream.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server classifies each `/v1/messages` request into `auxiliary` (no tools) / `compaction` (compact.ts prompt markers) / `tool_followup` (last msg has tool_result) / `normal`. Wrapper drops auxiliary entirely, streams compaction as `thinking_delta`, emits a result only on `normal` `end_turn`. Response-content fingerprint backup (`<analysis>` + Primary-Request-and-Intent + Pending-Tasks) catches anything that slipped past the request-level classifier.
- **Risk:** spilled-prompt overflow file leaks to concurrent runs via shared tempdir + `--add-dir`.
  - **Mitigation:** per-run subdir under `${tmpdir()}/openclaw-interactive-proxy/run-<32-hex-bytes>/` (0700), file 0600, `--add-dir` scoped to the per-run subdir only.
- **Risk:** local CA + leaf key persisted under user's OpenClaw config dir could be reused if an attacker gets read access.
  - **Mitigation:** trust scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` only (no system trust mutation); CA issues solely the one `api.anthropic.com` leaf the per-turn proxy needs; key files hardened to 0600 with fail-closed regeneration; maintainer security review invited.
- **Risk:** Anthropic-side non-SSE 4xx/5xx on `/v1/messages` (429, billing, overloaded) hangs the wrapper because claude doesn't self-exit on API error.
  - **Mitigation:** mitm-server emits a synthetic `api_error` event for non-SSE 4xx/5xx; wrapper surfaces a `result` with `is_error: true` and exits nonzero so the CLI runner treats the turn as failed and triggers failover.
- **Risk:** two resumes of the same Claude session race on the same transcript.
  - **Mitigation:** backend config sets `serialize: true` so the framework queues same-session runs; different sessions still run concurrently. mitm-server binds both proxy stages on port 0 so concurrent proxies can't collide on a preferred port either.

## Security Impact

- **New permissions/capabilities:** Yes — per-turn local TLS MITM on a loopback port. Scoped to the spawned claude child via `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS`; system trust store untouched. Local CA + leaf key cached under user's OpenClaw config dir at 0700/0600.
- **Secrets/tokens handling changed:** No — wrapper does not read, log, or modify the Anthropic auth token; it is forwarded byte-for-byte to the re-issued upstream request.
- **New/changed network calls:** Yes — claude's outbound HTTPS to `api.anthropic.com:443` is rerouted via `HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:<port>` inside the child. Non–`api.anthropic.com` CONNECT tunnels (statsig, datadog, etc.) bypass the TLS terminator entirely.
- **Command/tool execution surface changed:** No — same `claude` binary, same allowed-tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`), same session semantics.
- **Data access scope changed:** No — proxy sees only the SSE bytes claude itself sees on the wire; nothing exfiltrated or aggregated outside the per-turn process.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible: existing `claude-cli` backend unchanged. Opt-in via the `claude-cli-interactive` id; no default flips.
- New optional env: `OPENCLAW_INTERACTIVE_PROXY_DEBUG=1` enables verbose wrapper stderr.
- Migration needed: no.

## Prior art

[`smithersai/claude-p`](https://github.com/smithersai/claude-p) runs `claude` without `-p` but bypasses the JSONL streaming surface entirely — works for the raw final text but loses the `stream_event` records that reasoning and tool-use rendering depend on. This PR reproduces the `-p --output-format stream-json` shape record-for-record from the SSE tap so existing OpenClaw stream consumers layer on unchanged.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection, though I did not execute the repro in this read-only review. Current main still merges cached modelOverride/providerOverride fields over the fresh store row, and the PR body supplies terminal proof through production on-disk session-store paths.

target.title: Fix: preserve modelOverride in agent handler (#5369)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes the intermittent race condition where sub-agent model overrides were silently ignored (~3% failure rate).

- When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value would clobber the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`.
- The fix removes the stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch` (incl. `modelOverride`, `providerOverride`, `cliSessionIds`, `cliSessionBindings`, `claudeCliSessionId`, gated `pluginOwnerId`). `mergeSessionEntry` already preserves anything not in the patch, so fresh store values survive the write.
- Rotation-clearing for `sessionFile` (added by #79076) is preserved through a conditional spread that only writes `sessionFile: undefined` on session rotation.

## Root Cause

The agent handler was:
1. Reading the session entry early via `loadSessionEntry()` and holding it in a local variable.
2. Building `nextEntryPatch` with `modelOverride: entry?.modelOverride` and several siblings — values read from that captured local entry, not the fresh store.
3. Calling `updateSessionStore()` whose mutator does `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` — which spreads `{...freshStore, ...patch}`, so any stale value in the patch clobbers the fresh store value written concurrently (e.g., by `sessions.patch` setting `modelOverride` on a sub-agent between spawn and run).

## Changes

- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts`: drop stale `entry?.X` writebacks from `nextEntryPatch`. Switch the patch type to `Partial<SessionEntry>`. Gate `pluginOwnerId` behind `entry === undefined`. Convert the `sessionFile` rotation-clearing from #79076 into a conditional spread that fires only when `entry.sessionId !== sessionId`.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`: add the original #5369 regression and a broader regression guard for the whole stale-writeback class (sendPolicy, skillsSnapshot, thinkingLevel, fastMode, verboseLevel, traceLevel, reasoningLevel, systemSent, spawnedWorkspaceDir, spawnDepth, cliSessionIds, cliSessionBindings, claudeCliSessionId).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When `sessions.patch` writes a fresh `modelOverride` between when the agent handler captures its local `entry` reference and when it builds `nextEntryPatch`, the captured stale value clobbers the fresh store value through `mergeSessionEntry({...freshStore, ...patch})`. Per #5369, this manifested as ~3% of sub-agent runs silently ignoring an explicit model override and falling back to the parent's model.

**Real environment tested:**
- Host: Linux 6.14.0-34-generic on x86_64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw production session-store modules from this branch (`src/config/sessions/store.ts`, `src/config/sessions/types.ts`).
- Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant`: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, IP 10.119.193.216, Node v22.22.2, same modules from this branch via a host mount at `/home/ubuntu/openclaw-pr5369`.

Both run the production `loadSessionStore` / `updateSessionStore` / `mergeSessionEntry` code paths against a real on-disk JSON store in a per-run tmp directory. No mocking. No shims.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
node --import tsx scripts/repro-5369.mts 1000
```

The driver lives in this branch (local-only, intentionally not committed). For each of 1000 iterations it:

1. Initializes a session entry on disk via `updateSessionStore` (no override yet).
2. Captures the entry via `loadSessionStore({ skipCache: true })` — the same shape the agent handler holds in its local `entry` variable.
3. Fires a concurrent `updateSessionStore` to set `modelOverride: haiku-4.5-iter-N` (mimicking what `sessions.patch` does during sub-agent setup).
4. Builds `nextEntryPatch` two ways and applies each through the same `mergeSessionEntry(store[primaryKey], nextEntryPatch)` call shape that `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts` uses:
   - PRE-FIX shape: `modelOverride: capturedEntry.modelOverride` (writes back the captured stale `undefined`).
   - POST-FIX shape: `modelOverride` dropped from the patch — `mergeSessionEntry` preserves the fresh store value.
5. Reads the persisted entry back from disk and reports whether the override survived.

**Evidence after fix:**

Host (Linux 6.14, Node v22.22.0):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.0 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-0fZPH1/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (338 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (254 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

Multipass guest VM `ai-assistant` (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Node v22.22.2):

```
[repro #5369] node v22.22.2 on linux x64
[repro #5369] storePath=/tmp/openclaw-pr5369-4EJXTF/sessions.json
[repro #5369] iterations=1000

[repro #5369] running PRE-FIX patch shape (entry?.modelOverride writeback)...
[repro #5369] PRE-FIX :  1000/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (2602 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=undefined

[repro #5369] running POST-FIX patch shape (modelOverride dropped from patch)...
[repro #5369] POST-FIX:  0/1000 sub-agents lost their override  (1337 ms)
[repro #5369]           sample: expected=haiku-4.5-iter-0 actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0

[repro #5369] verdict: fix VERIFIED
```

**Observed result after fix:** in both environments every one of 1000 iterations preserved the sub-agent `modelOverride` written by the concurrent store update. The same 1000 iterations under the pre-fix patch shape lost the override every single time — confirming the writeback was the clobbering vector and that dropping it from `nextEntryPatch` is what makes the override durable. The persisted on-disk values (`actual=haiku-4.5-iter-0`) match what `sessions.patch` wrote, end-to-end, through the production merge code.

The 100% pre-fix loss rate (vs the ~3% reported in #5369) reflects that the driver deterministically forces the stale-then-fresh ordering on every iteration. Production traffic only hits that ordering when `sessions.patch` lands in the narrow window between the agent handler's `loadSessionEntry` and its own `updateSessionStore` call — about 3% of the time per the linked issue. Whenever that ordering does occur in production, the driver shows the pre-fix shape always loses the override and the post-fix shape never does.

**What was not tested:** end-to-end Anthropic API calls burning credit on each of the 1000 iterations. The sub-agent dispatch loop is upstream of the `modelOverride` persistence the fix addresses; a real model loop would only confirm what the persisted `modelOverride` value is, which the driver already reads back from disk on every iteration. Driving 1000 real Anthropic calls would not provide additional information about the persistence behavior the fix changes.

Fixes #5369

Supersedes #8398 (rebased onto latest main to resolve conflicts).
